text: Fix the live honesty defects the deep review (2026-06-11-04) and gaps G13/G15 surfaced, and prevent their recurrence. lume's README leads with an [E]/[U] evidence-tagging policy, so drift in those tagged claims is a self-inflicted breach of the project's own rule. Concretely: (1) hand-authored counts have drifted across three reviews - '313 passing tests' vs actual 427, 'eight closed workstreams, a ninth in progress' vs 22 closed, adopter wording vs ADOPTERS.json; (2) the live-install caveat is tagged [U] in README/docs/vision although workstream 0011's retro (P5.4) records an operator-run live install in the tredl repo and even claims the [U]->[E] edit landed (it never did) - an internally self-contradicting state; (3) the README still asserts 'It is v0.1.0' though 0.2.0 is released/tagged/merged; (4) docs/scope.md:64 (G15) denies decomposition/nesting was built although lume spawn ships and was accepted in 0011 P19-P22. Done-when: every drifted count/version/adopter claim in README.md and docs/*.md is corrected against derived reality; the live-install caveat is flipped [U]->[E] citing the 0011 retro (internal evidence, not external validation); the scope.md decomposition denial is corrected to acknowledge the shipped build; and a deterministic, offline CI guard - extending the existing version-consistency test pattern - re-derives test/workstream/adopter counts from source and fails when README/docs prose disagrees, so [E] counts cannot silently rot again. No engine behaviour change beyond the guard/test; suite green.
title: Re-anchor drifted README/docs claims and wire a CI freshness guard
